item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I for sure like how the article adds a lot to wondertainment, and gives a little more information abot an otherwise mysterious company.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hope the shipping is fast as light if it is I wish we has this in real life

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was almost sleeping, so not my best comment.

Let me make a point-to-point analysis.

1 It should be Euclid. Both the possibility of unknown properties and of remote tampering from Wondertainment keep it a little out from the "closed box" test for Safe. Yet, it is a minor thing.

2 Special Containment Procedures that are, actually, standardized. At least some protocols for research could be here. That is moderately bad.

3 I think -2 is too boring. It is a black box. A bland black box that can't be examined for reasons. Outside from being focused as the source of the anomaly, it actually does nothing besides being boring. The skip could work with the weird engine being directly connected to the weird screen. This is pretty bad.

4 Login is also boring. And excessive. If it were excessive and strange, that would be cool. Excessive and boring seems more like Prometheus Lab stuff. The same goes for the protective gear.

5 Overall the tone isn't quite as clinical as I expected, like Wondertainment weren't something anomalous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a weird, contentless thing to say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know. I love some Wondertainment, but there are several places where the article seems off to me. I'll try to come back later, but downvote for now.

item.title: JUNIOR STAFF POST OPEN - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired, edits implemented.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Proposed Edits
- Proposed Edits

A crosslink is being proposed to Site-81 as it is referenced in SCP-2445's containment procedures, and that the Site-81 dossier links back to SCP-2445. In addition, the author of SCP-2445 is also the author of the dossier for Site-81.

Editing this:

SCP-2445 is to be kept within the AVB (Anomalous Vehicle Bay) at Site-81.

To this:

SCP-2445 is to be kept within the AVB (Anomalous Vehicle Bay) at Site-81.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always love reading Wondertainment articles, they're quirky in the right ways and leave me laughing like slap stick humor cartoons do. Definitely an upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait so is the truck the standard delivery vehicle for Wondertainment products or is it a Wondertainment product in itself? The ending seems to imply the latter but I'm still not quite sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The truck could move at lightspeed. Who's to say the collects wont as well? I mean yeah sure try but I don't think it will work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the Foundation should do next is keep a 24/7 armed watch on the vehicle so nobody can show up to "collect" it. While that's going on, have people working on hacking the system so they can log in again. Perhaps with higher privileges this time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
moose's feedback: "this article is good but have you thought about adding some author avatars and like twenty crosslinks?"

anyway, yay for the edits! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Edits made per these suggestions. Honestly, I need to just start running all of my articles by you first, Moose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really nitpicky critique time.

+ show block
– hide block

Any personnel seeking to perform testing on SCP-2445 must have permission from a Level 3 staff member or above.

Why have this line? It's pointless and adds word count.

SCP-2445 is a white, mostly unmarked tractor trailer hosting a number of anomalous characteristics

Why specify "hosting a number of anomalous characteristics"?

The exterior of SCP-2445 displays no anomalous properties whatsoever, either on the truck or the trailer.

Why specify this? This sentence is actually a bigger problem because that paragraph is already skim bait; it's thick and hard to get through.

Beneath the engine hood of SCP-2445's truck section, however, is what appears to be a modified engine component

"however" and "what appears to be" are word cruft. Cut 'em.

It's also at this point that I start wondering why SCP-2445-1 and SCP-2445-2 have individual names. There's no reason they shouldn't, except that it makes the SCP description much harder to read. People are gonna basically skip these two paragraphs because of it.

Also, they're arbitrary designations: why isn't the control module -3? Why aren't all three components -1? What about the helmet and HUD? (The answer, of course, is that it would ruin the flow of the article, but the flow is already messed up.)

Activates a mechanism that, by currently unknown means, renders the entirety of SCP-2445 invisible to the naked eye.

"by currently unknown means" is word cruft. That's true for everything here; no need to specify.

Discovery Information:

You should break up this paragraph. Too much of a text wall.

– hide block

The rest of this is pretty nice. Actually, overall this is pretty neat. I enjoy Clarence, the dialogue, the note to the Foundation, the entertaining anomalous functions. +1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I adore this. Most of the Wondertainment pieces do. It fits perfectly with my headcanon that they would employ "normal" people outside of the corgi-filled corporate offices. The tone is excellent; dry and serious versus Prince's "Crap, am I gonna get a ticket?"

ONE question though. There has been no mention of transmissions to/from the vehicle. Yet Wondertainment not only knows the vehicle is in foundation custody (thus the "not so fast" when they try to take it "home"), but what Prince did to cause the accident. Has the good Doctor figured out a new way of wormholing(sp) communications?

+1 regardless. How do I send them my resume?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finally, we get to know how Wondertainment ships their products. For ages it's all been like "they show up out of nowhere". Well, they definitely did have to come from somewhere and a means to get there, no?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Not Applicable to this Ethnic Group!"

made me laugh for some reason.

WonderHome: Returns SCP-2445 to a main Dr. Wondertainment distribution hub. Option currently locked out, and attempting to access this option returns a "Not so fast!" notification.

I have no clue why, but I found that really creepy.

The incident logs at the end confused me, though. Did the Foundation let Prince go?

I also would have expected the Foundation to interrogate Prince for the locations of Wondertainment facilities. I'm sure the Foundation would have a lot of questions to ask Wondertainment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Wondertainment feel was captured really well here with all the amusing asides and theme naming, and I particularly enjoyed the understated Santa angle. All in all a nice, fresh take on Wondertainment. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do like a bit of insight into the mechanics of Wondertainment, but I'm also glad that it doesn't expose too much, and take all the fun out of it.

Also, picture's borked. I've reloaded it without effect, so I don't think it's just me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this SCP, particularly because they actually captured the driver and took him into custody. I'm so used to SCPs where the creator/people involved "mysteriously disappear" or "vanish without a trace" after the Foundation gets involved that it starts to get tiresome.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty much perfect. Well executed, and an article that adds a lot to the world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. An answer of how they get their products everywhere;P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's all right. Everyone makes silly mistakes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
WonderSpatialDistortionFixer

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, sorry. I didn't know that was a thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uh… Why would Light Emitting Diodes connect anything to anything? Leads connect electrical stuff to other electrical stuff, LEDs glow when electricity flows through them and don't do much else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love my some Wondertainment. The good doctor can have my +1 anyday.

item.title: Awesome
item.content:encoded: This is absolutely GREAT! It's whimsical and has the right feel for a Wondertainment article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is novel and well written, but it kind of rubs the the wrong way. I don't know if I like EVERY aspect of a GoI needing to be in some way anomalous. Why can't they have regular truck drivers and shipping routes and schedules? Still, it's well done, so no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shouldn't that be "back end?"

Could be a result of an auto-dictation software. Assuming I have his accent right, "end" could sound almost the same as "in," and the people reviewing the transcription got sloppy. It can happen, even in the Foundation.

Remember: humans aren't perfect. Therefore, the Foundation will never run perfectly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
back in jackknifed

Shouldn't that be "back end?"

Love this, though. I love how Wondertainment things are always so cheery. "Not so fast, Foundation goons!" Ouch. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and a helmet covered in 20 different leads

I think that should read "20 different LEDs."

Also, I love how they busted Clarence at the end there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this brand new look at an otherwise stale concept. Here, take my upvote. :)

item.title: Re: Wondertruckment is a bad name.
item.content:encoded: It has a certain charm. Shame about the driver, though.

item.title: Re: Wondertruckment is a bad name.
item.content:encoded: Maybe… Wondershipment would be better?

item.title: Wondertruckment is a bad name.
item.content:encoded:
This was essentially the product of a discussion with Djoric about the wiki only having articles about Wondertainment toys, and nothing else about the GOI. Shit, they'd have to have a way to deliver those toys, right?

And here we are.

Thanks again to the people who checked this out in chat, namely TwistedGears, Bouncl, Kalinin, Adelia, and anybody else I'm missing. I appreciate it!

As always, your comments, critiques, and criticisms are always welcome :D

Edit: image is from here https://p0.pxfuel.com/preview/1013/205/186/truck-american-transport-classical.jpg and is published under CC Zero (Public Domain).
